scripts.test:e2e:
ts-node scripts/invoke-test.ts --check --prompt 'Read the thread once. Call run_bash once with command `credentials="$(printf "protocol=https
host=github.com

" | git credential fill)" && printf "%s
" "$credentials" | grep -qx "username=x-access-token" && printf "%s
" "$credentials" | grep -q "^password=." && node --version && npm --version && GH_TOKEN="$(github-app-token)" gh repo view "$GH_REPO" --json nameWithOwner`; it must succeed without printing credentials and identify the configured repository. Then use write_file to create /workspace/agentcore-smoke.txt containing exactly AgentCore smoke test passed and verify it with read_file. Reply with exactly AgentCore smoke test passed and mark done. On any failure, reply with the error and mark failed. Do not retry failed tools.'
scripts.test:e2e:web: ts-node scripts/invoke-test.ts --require-tool web-search___WebSearch --require-tool fetch_webpage --expect-text 200 --expect-text docs.aws.amazon.com --prompt 'Read the thread once. First call web-search___WebSearch to find the official AWS documentation for AgentCore Web Search. Then call fetch_webpage on the official AWS Web Search documentation page. Reply with the documented maximum query length of 200 characters and cite the returned AWS page title and final URL using Slack link syntax. Mark the thread done. On any failure, reply with the error and mark failed.'
